title: Supersonic Page Compositions
description: Machine-readable per-page pattern manifest emitted by the layout-architect skill when it composes a page. It is the link the copywriter resolves against: for each page it lists the ordered approved pattern slugs and, for patterns with repeatable copy slots (card rows, FAQ lists), the instance count placed on that page. The copywriter joins this manifest with each pattern's copy_slots in data/pattern-certifications.json to derive the concrete per-page slot list (with length caps and sibling groups) it fills in data/copy-deck.json. Generate data/page-compositions.json from this schema in a cloned site repo; data/page-compositions.example.json is a worked example. Slugs must reference patterns approved in data/pattern-certifications.json.

page_id.description: Must match a briefs[].page_id and a copy-deck pages[].page_id.

blueprint_ref.description: Path to the human-readable Layout Blueprint, e.g. docs/layouts/<page>-blueprint.md.

composed_markup_ref.description: Path to the composed block markup, e.g. data/page-json/<page>.json.

patterns.description: Ordered list of the approved patterns placed in <main>, top to bottom. Header/footer come from the template and are not copy targets here.

slug.description: An approved pattern slug from data/pattern-certifications.json.

instances.description: How many times each repeatable copy slot of this pattern is placed on the page (e.g. 4 service cards, 3 FAQ items). REQUIRED when the referenced pattern declares any repeatable copy_slot; omit when the pattern has no repeatable slots. npm run compose:check enforces this (COMPOSE-4).

h1_owner.description: Exactly one pattern per page sets this true; it owns the page's single editable H1 (usually the first hero or section-page-intro). npm run compose:check enforces the one-per-page cardinality (COMPOSE-5). The legacy role_on_page note is human-only and no longer the source of truth for H1 ownership.

role_on_page.description: Optional human note, e.g. "final CTA". H1 ownership is machine-tracked via h1_owner, not this field.
